concerns.text: T012 and T013 (US1 Implementation) depend on the canonical synthetic data generator (T005) for the fallback path described in the plan. However, T012 is listed as [P] (parallel-safe) alongside T013. T013 (TPM normalization/imputation) logically requires the output of T012 (ingested data) to operate on. T012 and T013 cannot run in parallel; T013 must strictly follow T012. Marking T013 as [P] is a semantic violation of the producer-consumer flow.

T014 (Merge), T015 (Replicate check), and T016 (Metadata check) all consume the output of T013 (normalized/imputed data). T014 is marked [P], but it cannot execute until T013 completes. Furthermore, T015 and T016 are listed as separate tasks but are logically part of the merge/validation step in T014. If T014 is [P], it implies it can run in parallel with T013, which is impossible. The dependency chain T012 -> T013 -> T014 is broken by the [P] tags.

T020 and T021 (US2 Training) depend on the merged dataset produced by T017 (US1 output). T020 is marked [P]. While T020 can run in parallel with other US2 tasks (like T022), it cannot run in parallel with T017. The [P] tag on T020 is acceptable only if interpreted as 'parallel with other US2 tasks', but the phase structure implies US2 starts after US1. The current labeling is ambiguous and risks a consumer-before-producer error if a runner attempts to start T020 before T017 finishes.

T028 and T029 (US3 Interpretation) depend on the trained model artifact from T024 (US2). T028 is marked [P]. Similar to US2, T028 cannot run until T024 completes. The [P] tag is misleading here as it suggests independence from the model training phase, violating the strict artifact-flow dependency (Model -> Interpretation).

T010 and T011 are marked [P] and listed as 'Tests for User Story 1'. The spec requires tests to be written FIRST and fail before implementation. However, T010 (Contract test for ingestion) and T011 (Integration test for merge) depend on the existence of the code in T012 and T014 to be testable (or at least the schema definitions). If T010 is a contract test, it might be independent, but T011 (merge logic) clearly depends on T014. The [P] tag on T011 is likely incorrect if it implies parallel execution with T014 implementation.
